svg.title: Group velocity versus phase velocity: carrier wave and beat envelope at two times
svg.desc: Two stacked snapshots of a matter-wave packet showing the fast carrier wave cos(kx) (ink, fine oscillations) and its slow beat envelope |cos(dk/2 * x)| (red, broad shape). Between the top panel at t=0 and the bottom panel at later time, the envelope peak (group velocity arrow, longer) has advanced twice as far as the carrier phase pattern (phase velocity arrow, shorter), with a vertical dashed reference line at the initial envelope peak position making the differential displacement explicit. For de Broglie matter waves v_g = 2*v_ph = v_particle.

cajal:figure.figure-title: Group velocity vs phase velocity: carrier and envelope
cajal:figure.author: Humanitarians AI Incorporated

svg.text: Group velocity v𝑔 = 2vₚₕ for de Broglie matter wave (omega = hbar k²/2m)
